Authentication Session Policy

Users should be asked to sign in again when their session is no longer trusted.
Examples include long inactivity, device change, password reset, administrator
revocation, and failed refresh checks.

The policy focuses on user experience and risk. Short sessions improve safety
but can interrupt work; long sessions reduce friction but increase exposure if a
device is lost.

This document is a semantic neighbor for timeout and sign-in questions even when
the query does not name a particular token format.
